title: Venezuelan Phyllo Crumble
description: A rich, crumbly dessert made with layers of phyllo dough, nuts, and cheese, baked to perfection in a cast-iron skillet. Perfect for a dessert or snack.
instructions: Thaw the phyllo dough according to the package instructions.
Layer 4-5 sheets of phyllo dough in the bottom of a 10-inch cast-iron skillet, brushing each sheet with melted butter.
Sprinkle 1/4 cup of granulated sugar and 1/4 cup of chopped walnuts over the top of the phyllo dough.
Repeat the layering process 2-3 more times, ending with a layer of phyllo dough on top.
Mix the heavy cream and egg substitute together in a bowl until well combined.
Pour the cream mixture over the top of the phyllo dough.
Sprinkle the shredded parmesan cheese over the top of the cream mixture.
Cover the skillet with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the phyllo dough is crispy.
